translation_1: Let's cross the road together.
translation_2: He crossed the road.
translation_3: He crossed to that side.

definition: to go over (exercises, a document, etc.)
sentence_1: 老师让我们过一遍这些字。
translation_1: The teacher asked us to go over these characters once.
translation_2: Let me go over these questions again.
translation_3: Go over these books once before the exam.

translation_1: I spent two days at home.
translation_2: Where did you spend your birthday?
sentence_3: 我们一起过了一个快乐的下午。
translation_3: We spent a happy afternoon together.

definition: used after a verb to indicate completion (e.g. 我去过中国。 I have been to China)
translation_1: I have been to Beijing twice.
translation_2: Have you eaten this dish before?
translation_3: She has studied Chinese before.

definition: used after an adjective to indicate a prior state (e.g. 他小时候胖过。 He was fat when he was little.)
translation_1: He was fat when he was little.
translation_2: She was pretty when she was young.
translation_3: This place used to be cold; it's not cold anymore.

translation_1: Come over here for a moment.
translation_2: She came over from that side.
translation_3: Come over quickly; we're leaving.

definition: used after verb to indicate sufficiency of time, ability, or quantity
translation_1: The food isn't much; I can finish it all.
translation_2: There aren't too many things; I can carry them all.
translation_3: There aren't too many people; I can handle them all.

translation_1: Where do you celebrate Chinese New Year?
translation_2: Our family celebrates the New Year together.
sentence_3: 过年的时候我们吃饺子。
translation_3: When we celebrate Chinese New Year, we eat dumplings.

translation_1: In former times he loved studying.
translation_2: In former times there were no buildings here.
translation_3: She was a student in former times.

translation_1: Don't think too much about the past.
translation_2: He misses the past very much.
translation_3: In the past, we lived there.

sentence_1: 一辆车从我们旁边过去了。
translation_1: A car passed by us.
translation_2: He passed by the doorway.
translation_3: Let's pass by over there.

translation_1: You had better go to sleep early.
translation_2: It's cold; you had better wear more.
translation_3: It's far; you had better take a car.

translation_1: It rained, but I went nevertheless.
translation_2: He was very tired, but he finished it nevertheless.
translation_3: Mom didn't allow it, but she went nevertheless.

translation_1: Will you drink tea or water?
translation_2: Are you going or not?
translation_3: Is he your older brother or younger brother?

translation_1: I like black clothes.
translation_2: That cat is black.
translation_3: This bag is black.

translation_1: I want to drink a cup of black tea.
translation_2: She drinks black tea every morning.
translation_3: This cup of black tea is very good.

sentence_1: 她穿了一件红色的衣服。
translation_1: She wore a red piece of clothing.
translation_2: I like red.
translation_3: That bag is red.

translation_1: The book is at the back of the table.
translation_2: He is sitting at the back, behind me.
translation_3: The dog is at the back of the door.

translation_1: They are walking in the rear.
translation_2: I'll wait for you in the rear.
translation_3: He is at the very rear.

translation_1: This flower is very pretty.
translation_2: She likes flowers.
translation_3: There are flowers on the table.

translation_1: I spent fifty yuan.
translation_2: How much money did you spend on books?
translation_3: She spent a lot of time.

translation_1: This painting is very beautiful.
translation_2: There is a painting on the table.
